What is ESG Assurance?

ESG Assurance is an independent verification process that confirms the accuracy, credibility, and completeness of your sustainability disclosures, whether it’s your Greenhouse Gas (GHG) inventory, Business Responsibility and Sustainability Report (BRSR), or Annual Sustainability Report (ASR).

Using globally recognized standards like the AA1000 Standard (AA1000AS v3), third-party validates your data and builds stakeholder confidence. It also prepares your organization for regulatory scrutiny, investor evaluation, and global sustainability rankings. At Growlity, we offer independent assurance services tailored to your sustainability reporting needs, delivering credibility, transparency, and audit readiness across your journey.

Gap Assessment

A detailed gap analysis to benchmark your climate disclosure performance and create a tailored roadmap to improve your Carbon Disclosure project (CDP) score.

Build a roadmap with targeted steps to enhance your Carbon Disclosure project (CDP) disclosure.

ESG Benchmarking

Evaluate current practices against Carbon Disclosure project (CDP)scoring criteria and peer disclosures.

Readiness Insights

Identify disclosure gaps and key priorities before your next Carbon Disclosure project (CDP) submission.

Climate Change

FAQs

On The Side of Ambitious

 Action 

We help brands stand out and grow by creating innovative, results-driven strategies that engage customers and drive success.

What is the Carbon Disclosure Project (CDP)?

The Carbon Disclosure Project (CDP) is a globally recognized non-profit organization that operates the world’s leading environmental disclosure system. It enables companies, cities, states, and regions to measure and manage their environmental impacts by reporting data related to climate change, water security, and deforestation. Through CDP, organizations can enhance transparency, track progress toward sustainability goals, and build trust with stakeholders by aligning with best practices in environmental responsibility.

What are the Carbon Disclosure project (CDP) scoring levels (A to D)?

Carbon Disclosure project (CDP) assigns scores based on the quality and completeness of a company’s environmental disclosures. The scoring methodology categorizes responses into four key levels: A (Leadership): Demonstrates best practice in environmental management and strategic integration into business operations. B (Management): Shows awareness of environmental issues and a structured approach to managing them through policies and initiatives. C (Awareness): Reflects recognition of environmental challenges and basic understanding of associated risks and opportunities. D/D- (Disclosure): Indicates transparency in providing environmental data, but without significant environmental governance or action plans. Scoring is done separately for Climate Change, Water Security, and Forests (if applicable), and the goal is to support continuous improvement over time.

Is Carbon Disclosure project (CDP) reporting mandatory?

No, Carbon Disclosure project (CDP) reporting is not legally required; however, it has become a de facto requirement in many industries due to growing pressure from investors, customers, and regulatory bodies. Voluntary CDP participation can give organizations a competitive advantage by demonstrating commitment to sustainability, improving access to green finance, meeting ESG requirements, and preparing for future regulations. Increasingly, large corporations and financial institutions request their suppliers and partners to disclose through CDP.

Which companies should report to Carbon Disclosure project (CDP) ?

Any organization with significant environmental impact—especially in sectors like manufacturing, energy, transport, agriculture, and consumer goods—should consider Carbon Disclosure project (CDP) reporting. In particular, companies that are publicly traded, have high greenhouse gas emissions, rely on water-intensive operations, or source from forest-risk commodities are encouraged to participate. Additionally, businesses that are part of Carbon Disclosure project (CDP) Supply Chain programs, where customers request disclosures from suppliers, should respond to maintain their commercial relationships and improve ESG transparency.

Can you help us improve our Carbon Disclosure project (CDP) score?

Absolutely. Our sustainability consultants specialize in Carbon Disclosure project (CDP) reporting and performance improvement. We begin by analyzing your previous Carbon Disclosure project (CDP) submissions, identifying scoring gaps, and aligning your disclosures with Carbon Disclosure project (CDP)’s latest scoring methodology and guidance documents. We also help structure your environmental strategy, implement best practices, and gather credible documentation to elevate your performance. Whether you’re starting with a Disclosure-level score or aiming to reach Leadership status, we offer end-to-end support to help you move up the scoring scale with confidence.

Explore More

You might also Be Interested In…!!!

Ecovadis Assessment

EcoVadis is a globally recognized sustainability assessment platform that evaluates a company’s performance across four key pillars: Environment, Labor u0026 Human Rights, Ethics, and Sustainable Procurement. Over 150,000 companies worldwide rely on EcoVadis to benchmark, monitor, and improve their ESG ratings across global supply chains, making it one of the most trusted platforms for sustainability performance. As an EcoVadis consultant and approved training partner, Growlity supports organizations in successfully completing their EcoVadis assessment, improving their EcoVadis score, and strengthening their sustainability credibility. Our tailored consulting ensures higher ESG performance, enhanced compliance, and greater stakeholder confidence, positioning your business for long-term, responsible growth.

Read More

Ecovadis Assessment

ESG Reporting

ESG Reporting refers to the structured disclosure of a company’s Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) performance. It enables businesses to demonstrate how they manage risks and opportunities across climate change, resource efficiency, human rights, ethical governance, diversity, and more. In today’s investor-driven, regulation-focused environment, ESG reporting is no longer optional—it’s a strategic imperative. ESG disclosures influence investment decisions, consumer trust, regulatory compliance, and corporate reputation.

Read More

ESG Reporting

Lifecycle Assessment (LCA)

A lifecycle assessment is a comprehensive, ISO‑standardized method that quantifies environmental impacts across a product’s entire existence—from raw material extraction through manufacturing, usage, and disposal. Known under global standards ISO 14040 and ISO 14044, LCA enables businesses to make informed decisions on design, strategy, and eco‑innovation. At Growlity, we provide rigorous, ISO‑aligned life cycle impact assessment to help manufacturers, brands, and suppliers measure, optimize, and communicate your environmental performance credibly.

Read More

Lifecycle Assessment (LCA)
